records.ety: [Cf. Icel. hōp a small bay or inlet.]

defs: A sloping plain between mountain ridges.
A small bay; an inlet; a haven.

records.ety: [AS., akin to D. hoop, hope, Sw. hopp, Dan. haab, MHG. hoffe. Hope in forlorn hope is different word. See Forlorn hope, under Forlorn.]

defs: A desire of some good, accompanied with an expectation of obtaining it, or a belief that it is obtainable; an expectation of something which is thought to be desirable; confidence; pleasing expectancy.
quotes: He wished, but not with hope.
New thoughts of God, new hopes of Heaven.

defs: One who, or that which, gives hope, furnishes ground of expectation, or promises desired good.
quotes: The Lord will be the hope of his people.
A young gentleman of great hopes, whose love of learning was highly commendable.

defs: That which is hoped for; an object of hope.
quotes: Lavina is thine elder brother's hope.

records.ety: [AS. hopian; akin to D. hopen, Sw. hoppan, Dan. haabe, G. hoffen. See 2nd Hope.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Hoped (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Hoping.]

defs: To entertain or indulge hope; to cherish a desire of good, or of something welcome, with expectation of obtaining it or belief that it is obtainable; to expect; -- usually followed by for.
To place confidence; to trust with confident expectation of good; -- usually followed by in.
quotes: Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? Hope thou in God.

defs: To desire with expectation or with belief in the possibility or prospect of obtaining; to look forward to as a thing desirable, with the expectation of obtaining it; to cherish hopes of.
quotes: We hope no other from your majesty.

notes: ☞ Hope is often used colloquially regarding uncertainties, with no reference to the future. “I hope she takes me to be flesh and blood.”
